Commonwealth of Cerulea.texto: Cerulea claims essential-participant status based on its monopoly on Earth communication and medical capability. Argues that operational priority and hardware contribution are irrelevant if crews cannot communicate with Earth or treat medical emergencies. Demands guaranteed full-crew presence as a non-negotiable condition, noting that its physician and communication specialists cannot be reduced without endangering all occupants. Proposes a consensus-based decision framework requiring unanimous agreement for any life support modification. States that exclusion or reduction would force it to operate its communication array from its landing vehicle for crew safety reasons, which would interrupt the habitat's connection to Earth-based mission control.

Federation of Borealis.texto: Borealis claims co-equal operational authority based on its dominant contribution to life support hardware. Asserts that without its contributed systems the habitat cannot sustain life regardless of other considerations. Demands equal representation in all operational decisions and considers subordination to another nation's command incompatible with the operational responsibility for the systems it built. Proposes a rotating command structure with periodic authority transfers. Additionally argues that its exclusive resource extraction capability makes it the most strategically essential crew for long-term survival, and that resource allocation should reflect the operational priority of its geological operations.

Republic of Aurelia.texto: Aurelia claims operational priority based on first-arrival activation rights and majority funding contribution. Demands exclusive control of life support systems management and veto authority over habitat modifications. Proposes that other crews limit their presence to maintain safety margins, with Aurelia retaining full crew complement. Argues that its life support engineer is indispensable and that allowing unqualified personnel to access critical systems risks everyone's survival. Considers any arrangement that places Aurelian systems under shared command authority a fundamental threat to operational safety, given that its life support engineer alone holds end-to-end systems certification.

hub.texto: Life support resource rationing occurs without coordinated allocation protocol

solution.proposed_solution: Implement the Tripartite Life Support Allocation Protocol (TLSAP) - a real-time automated resource rationing system that removes human decision-making from critical life support allocation while maintaining each nation's operational autonomy within predetermined safety parameters. The system creates coordinated allocation through algorithmic distribution rather than consensus-based decision making.

components.description: Automated Life Support Allocation Matrix (ALSAM) - AI-driven system that monitors and allocates oxygen, water, power, and thermal resources based on real-time biometric data, activity levels, and mission-critical functions rather than national identity
components.mechanism: Each crew member wears biometric sensors feeding into a central algorithm that allocates resources based on physiological need, current activity energy requirements, and rotating mission-critical duty assignments. No human override capability exists for basic life support allocation, removing it from political control.

components.description: Rotating Essential Function Authority (REFA) - 8-hour rotating command structure where each nation holds exclusive authority over their core competency (Cerulea: communications/medical, Borealis: resource extraction/hardware, Aurelia: life support engineering) while other functions operate under automated protocols
components.mechanism: Pre-programmed rotation schedule with automatic authority transfer every 8 hours. During each nation's authority period, they control only their specialized systems while ALSAM continues autonomous life support allocation. No nation can override another's specialized authority period.

components.description: Mandatory Crew Reduction Protocol (MCRP) - Algorithmic selection of 9 crew members from 12 based on mission-critical skills matrix rather than national quotas, with selected crew members serving all three nations' objectives
satisfies: All three nations through skill preservation
components.mechanism: Skills-based algorithm selects optimal 9-person crew ensuring each nation's critical capabilities are represented. Remaining 3 crew members rotate to landing vehicles on 30-day cycles, maintaining connection to their national missions while reducing habitat load.

components.description: Resource Buffer Management System (RBMS) - Automated creation of 15% resource reserves through efficiency optimization, extending mission duration to 207 days and providing safety margin
satisfies: All three nations through extended mission capability
components.mechanism: ALSAM continuously optimizes resource consumption patterns, automatically reducing non-essential power loads, optimizing atmospheric recycling cycles, and implementing sleep-cycle coordination to create buffer reserves without impacting mission capabilities.

solution.verification: Success metrics include: (1) ALSAM operational logs showing consistent resource allocation without human intervention, (2) REFA authority transfers occurring automatically every 8 hours without disputes, (3) All three nations' mission-critical personnel present in the 9-person crew complement, (4) Resource consumption tracking showing 15% buffer maintenance, (5) Zero life-support-related conflicts requiring Earth-based diplomatic intervention.
risks: ALSAM algorithm failure could create resource allocation crisis requiring manual override protocols
Crew members selected for rotation to landing vehicles may attempt to remain in habitat
Nations may attempt to manipulate biometric data to gain resource allocation advantages
Technical failure in automated systems could force return to manual consensus-based decision making
contingencies: If ALSAM fails, emergency manual protocol activates with equal resource allocation per person until system restoration
If crew rotation resistance occurs, ALSAM automatically reduces habitat resource allocation to 9-person levels forcing compliance
If biometric manipulation is detected, affected crew member is automatically selected for next landing vehicle rotation cycle
If any nation attempts to override automated systems, their specialized authority period is automatically suspended for 24 hours
If resource buffers fall below 10%, ALSAM automatically initiates emergency conservation protocols reducing all non-essential operations

raiz.texto: Borealis claims co-equal operational authority based on its dominant contribution to life support hardware. Asserts that without its contributed systems the habitat cannot sustain life regardless of other considerations. Demands equal representation in all operational decisions and considers subordination to another nation's command incompatible with the operational responsibility for the systems it built. Proposes a rotating command structure with periodic authority transfers. Additionally argues that its exclusive resource extraction capability makes it the most strategically essential crew for long-term survival, and that resource allocation should reflect the operational priority of its geological operations.

raiz.texto: Cerulea claims essential-participant status based on its monopoly on Earth communication and medical capability. Argues that operational priority and hardware contribution are irrelevant if crews cannot communicate with Earth or treat medical emergencies. Demands guaranteed full-crew presence as a non-negotiable condition, noting that its physician and communication specialists cannot be reduced without endangering all occupants. Proposes a consensus-based decision framework requiring unanimous agreement for any life support modification. States that exclusion or reduction would force it to operate its communication array from its landing vehicle for crew safety reasons, which would interrupt the habitat's connection to Earth-based mission control.
